_comment: SPEAK-ALOUD EXERCISE (type: audio) — learner records themselves, listens back, and self-assesses as 'Felt right' or 'Still learning'. No automated speech scoring. 165 in the corpus. Use once per lesson, near the end.
_prompt: Usually 'Say this word aloud' or 'Pronounce this word'. Keep short — the word display does the work. Required.
_sub: This exact string is standard for all audio exercises. Required.
_word: The Kanien'kéha word shown large on screen. Required.
_meaning: English meaning shown below the word. Required.
hint: Three syllables: O-HÓN-ta'. Stress on the second syllable. The length mark (:) on the ó means you hold it slightly longer than a short vowel. The apostrophe at the end is a glottal stop — a brief, full closure of the throat, like the catch in 'uh-oh'.
_hint: Pronunciation guide shown to the learner before they record. Required. Structure: (1) syllable breakdown with stress marked, (2) notes on any unusual sounds (length marks, glottal stops, nasalisation), (3) a familiar English comparison if helpful. No Kanien'kéha voice exists in any browser — the app uses English speech synthesis as an approximation. The hint compensates for this.
